selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a young man living in Naples, I've noticed that social media has become a significant part of my daily life. I spend hours scrolling through feeds, comparing my life to others, and getting sucked into the vortex of fake news and propaganda. It's exhausting and demotivating, and I've begun to question the authenticity of everything I see online. One day, I stumbled upon a post claiming that a popular social media platform was censoring certain topics and opinions. I was skeptical, but I couldn't help but feel a twinge of outrage and anger. It wasn't until I dug deeper and fact-checked the information that I realized it was completely fabricated. This experience made me realize how easily I can be manipulated and how important it is to critically evaluate the information I consume online. To manage and deal with fake content on social media, I've started to take a few steps back. I've begun to limit my social media usage, especially during peak hours when I'm most susceptible to getting sucked into the vortex of misinformation. I've also started to follow reputable sources and fact-checking websites, and I've made a conscious effort to engage in critical thinking and media literacy. It's not always easy, and I sometimes find myself getting caught up in the emotional rollercoaster of online discourse. But I know that by being more mindful and critically thinking, I can avoid falling prey to the propaganda and misinformation that's so prevalent online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I realized that I was vulnerable to manipulation by fake news and propaganda on social media. I began to limit my social media usage and started to follow reputable sources and fact-checking websites. I also made a conscious effort to engage in critical thinking and media literacy. I understand that it's not always easy, but I know that by being more mindful, I can avoid falling prey to the misinformation that's so prevalent online. I also started to feel more connected to people in real life and less to people online, which helped me to reduce my anxiety and improve my mood.
citations: Source: Pew Research Center on the impact of social media on mental health
